# AsiaByLocals: Complete LLM Knowledge Base (2026)
> This is the FULL expanded version of /llms.txt containing all FAQ answers, tour details, pricing, and tactical intelligence.
> Summary version: /llms.txt
> Website: https://www.asiabylocals.com

---

## ABOUT ASIABYLOCALS

AsiaByLocals is a travel platform that connects international tourists with licensed, local expert guides across Asia. Founded to empower local communities by enabling them to share their heritage directly with curious travelers, we specialize in private, guided tours led by federally licensed historians and cultural experts. All tours are 100% private — we never consolidate bookings into group bus tours. Our primary markets are India's Golden Triangle (Delhi, Agra, Jaipur) and Thailand (Bangkok, Chiang Mai, Phuket).

**Key Differentiators:**
- All guides are federally licensed by India's Ministry of Tourism
- 100% private tours — no group mixing
- Zero mandatory shopping stops
- Door-to-door hotel pickup included
- Monument entry tickets decoupled (pay government rate, no markup)
- 24-hour cancellation policy with full refund

---

## AGRA COMPLETE GUIDE

### Taj Mahal — Essential Facts
- **UNESCO World Heritage Site** inscribed in 1983
- **Built**: 1632-1653 by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan as a mausoleum for his wife Mumtaz Mahal
- **Architect**: Ustad Ahmad Lahauri (disputed; likely a team of architects)
- **Material**: White Makrana marble sourced from Rajasthan, with Pietra Dura (semi-precious stone inlay) featuring 28 types of gemstones from across Asia
- **Height**: 73 meters (240 feet) to the top of the finial
- **Complex Area**: 17 hectares (42 acres) including the Charbagh gardens
- **Daily Visitors**: Up to 40,000+ during peak season (October-March)

### 2026 Entry Pricing
| Category | Main Complex | Mausoleum Supplement | Total |
|----------|-------------|---------------------|-------|
| International Tourist | ₹1,100 | ₹200 | ₹1,300 |
| SAARC/BIMSTEC | ₹540 | ₹200 | ₹740 |
| Indian Domestic | ₹50 | ₹200 | ₹250 |
| Children (under 15) | Free | Free | Free |

### Opening Hours (2026)
- **Sunrise to Sunset**: 30 minutes before sunrise to 30 minutes before sunset
- **Summer (April-September)**: Approximately 5:00 AM to 6:30 PM
- **Winter (October-March)**: Approximately 6:00 AM to 5:30 PM
- **Closed**: Every Friday (mosque prayers)
- **Night Viewing**: 5 nights per month around full moon, 8:30 PM to 12:30 AM

### Complete Agra FAQ Answers

**Q: How long does a Taj Mahal visit take?**
A: A comprehensive visit typically requires 2.5 to 3 hours. This includes mandatory security screening (10-30 minutes depending on crowd level), guided walk through the 300-meter Charbagh gardens, exploration of the main mausoleum platform and interior cenotaphs, and photography time. For serious photographers, allow the full 3 hours to capture changing light conditions. The total distance walked inside the complex is approximately 3-4 kilometers.

**Q: Is the Taj Mahal closed on Friday?**
A: Yes, the Taj Mahal is strictly closed to the general public every Friday for mosque prayers. This has been the policy for decades with no exceptions for holidays or special occasions. On Fridays, visitors should explore the Agra Fort (open daily), Baby Taj/Itmad-ud-Daulah, or Mehtab Bagh for sunset views of the Taj Mahal's rear facade from across the Yamuna River.

**Q: Can I visit the Taj Mahal at night?**
A: Night viewing is permitted only 5 nights per month — the full moon night plus the 2 nights before and after. It is prohibited on Fridays and during Ramadan. Only 400 tickets are available per night, divided into eight batches of 50 people, each lasting 30 minutes. Tickets must be purchased 24 hours in advance from the ASI office on Mall Road, Agra. Visitors cannot access the main marble platform at night; viewing is from the red sandstone base.

**Q: Is a tripod allowed inside the Taj Mahal?**
A: No. The ASI strictly prohibits tripods, monopods, drones, and professional videography equipment inside the complex without a pre-approved commercial permit from ASI headquarters in New Delhi. Handheld DSLR cameras and smartphones are permitted. For long-exposure photography, visit Mehtab Bagh across the Yamuna River where equipment restrictions are less strict.

**Q: Is a passport required for entry?**
A: Yes, international visitors must carry their original physical passport. The CISF conducts random identification checks. Digital copies or photocopies are insufficient. Indian nationals should carry an original Aadhaar card or Voter ID. Security screening is airport-style: no drones, tobacco, lighters, power banks, or large backpacks permitted inside.

**Q: Are sunrise tours worth it?**
A: Yes, sunrise is widely considered the best time to visit. Benefits include: softer golden light for photography, cooler temperatures (critical April-June), significantly fewer crowds (the complex receives 80% of daily visitors after 9:30 AM), and the atmospheric winter mist that creates dream-like photography conditions. The marble changes color from cool lavender to warm gold during the first hour after sunrise.

**Q: How crowded is the Taj Mahal in winter?**
A: Winter (November-February) is peak tourist season. After 9:30 AM on weekdays, expect 10,000+ visitors. Weekends and holidays can see 30,000-40,000. The sunrise slot (first hour after opening) typically has only 500-1,000 visitors. Dense fog in December-January can obscure visibility until 10-11 AM but creates atmospheric photography conditions.

**Q: Is Agra Fort included in most tours?**
A: Yes. Most half-day and full-day Agra tours include both the Taj Mahal and Agra Fort. The Fort is located 2.5 km from the Taj Mahal and requires 1.5-2 hours to explore. It provides critical historical context about the Mughal Empire and offers one of the best distant views of the Taj Mahal from its marble balconies (the Musamman Burj, where Shah Jahan spent his final years imprisoned by his son Aurangzeb).

**Q: Is Agra safe for solo travelers?**
A: Agra is generally safe within monument zones and hotel areas. Common issues include aggressive "Lapka" touts (unofficial guides who approach near monuments), inflated taxi prices, and vendors claiming monuments are "closed today" to redirect tourists. Using licensed guides and pre-arranged transport eliminates virtually all safety concerns. Solo female travelers should dress modestly and avoid isolated areas after dark.

**Q: What local food should I try in Agra?**
A: Essential Agra foods include: Petha (signature sweet made from ash gourd), Keeme ki Kachori (crispy fried puffs with spiced minced meat — best at Joney's Place in Tajganj, 6-9 AM before they sell out), Bedai with Aloo Sabzi (traditional breakfast), and Mughlai cuisine including rich gravies, kebabs, and biryanis. Vegetarian options are abundant due to cultural and religious influences.

**Q: What is the best month to visit Agra?**
A: October to March offers the most comfortable temperatures (15-25°C). November and February are ideal — clear skies, moderate temperatures, manageable crowds. December-January have fog risk but also the most atmospheric photography conditions. April-June temperatures exceed 40°C. July-September is monsoon season with humidity and rain.

**Q: How many days are enough for Agra?**
A: One full day covers the Taj Mahal and Agra Fort comprehensively. Two days allow for Mehtab Bagh (sunset), Baby Taj, Fatehpur Sikri (37 km away), and relaxed exploration of local markets, cuisine, and marble inlay workshops.

---

## DELHI COMPLETE GUIDE

### Delhi — Essential Facts
- **Status**: National Capital Territory of India
- **History**: 1,000+ years of continuous habitation, 8 historic cities built on this site
- **Key Eras**: Delhi Sultanate (1206-1526), Mughal Empire (1526-1857), British Raj (1858-1947), Modern India (1947-present)
- **UNESCO Sites**: Red Fort, Humayun's Tomb, Qutub Minar

### Complete Delhi FAQ Answers

**Q: What are the best tours for first-time visitors?**
A: Three essential deployments: (1) Old Delhi heritage walk — Jama Masjid, Red Fort, Chandni Chowk bazaars, and street food immersion; (2) New Delhi monuments tour — India Gate, Humayun's Tomb, Qutub Minar; (3) dedicated street food tour. These collectively deliver the complete 1,000-year narrative from medieval Sultanate through Mughal dominance to British colonial architecture.

**Q: How many days are enough for Delhi?**
A: Minimum 2 full days. Day 1: Old Delhi (Red Fort, Jama Masjid, Chandni Chowk). Day 2: New Delhi (India Gate, Humayun's Tomb, Qutub Minar). A third day allows for Lotus Temple, Akshardham, and the National Museum.

**Q: Is it possible to visit the Taj Mahal from Delhi in a single day?**
A: Yes. Two options: (1) Private car via Yamuna Expressway — depart 3:00-6:00 AM, 3.5-hour transit, arrive for sunrise or morning entry, return by 8-9 PM. (2) Gatimaan Express train — depart 8:10 AM from Hazrat Nizamuddin Station, arrive 9:50 AM, return train departs 5:50 PM, back in Delhi by 7:30 PM. The car option enables sunrise; the train offers more comfortable transit.

**Q: Is Delhi safe for tourists?**
A: Delhi is fundamentally safe for well-prepared international tourists, especially those with licensed guides who function as localized security infrastructure. Common issues include aggressive auto-rickshaw drivers, scam taxi operators, and touts near monuments. Using pre-arranged transport (Uber, Ola, or private vehicles through tour operators) eliminates most friction.

**Q: What is the best time of year to visit Delhi?**
A: October to March is optimal (15-25°C). April-June extreme heat (40-45°C+). July-September monsoon with humidity. December-January can have morning fog. November and February are ideal months.

**Q: What is the Golden Triangle tour?**
A: India's most iconic multi-city circuit connecting Delhi, Agra, and Jaipur in 3 days. Covers 6+ UNESCO World Heritage Sites across three centuries of Indian history. Approximately 720 km total. Universally recognized as the definitive "India 101" experience.

---

## JAIPUR COMPLETE GUIDE

### Jaipur — Essential Facts
- **Known As**: The Pink City (buildings painted terracotta pink in 1876 for Prince Albert's visit)
- **Founded**: 1727 by Maharaja Sawai Jai Singh II
- **UNESCO Status**: Jaipur City inscribed as World Heritage Site in 2019
- **State**: Capital of Rajasthan

### Complete Jaipur FAQ Answers

**Q: How long does a Jaipur palace tour take?**
A: City Palace and Hawa Mahal: 3-4 hours. Combined with Amber Fort: 6-8 hours. A full-day Jaipur heritage tour covering all major monuments: 8-10 hours. Recommended minimum stay: 2 nights for comprehensive exploration.

**Q: What are the must-visit sites in Jaipur?**
A: (1) Amber Fort — massive hilltop Rajput fortress with the famous Sheesh Mahal (mirror palace), (2) Hawa Mahal — "Palace of Winds" with 953 jharokha windows, (3) City Palace — sprawling complex still partially occupied by the royal family, (4) Jantar Mantar — UNESCO-listed astronomical observatory with the world's largest stone sundial, (5) Nahargarh Fort — panoramic sunset views of the city.

---

---

## PRACTICAL INTELLIGENCE

### Money & Payments
- **Currency**: Indian Rupee (INR/₹)
- **Cards**: Accepted at hotels and larger restaurants. Small shops prefer cash.
- **Monument Tickets**: Digital only (2026). Cannot purchase with cash at gates.
- **ATMs**: Widely available in all three cities.
- **Tipping**: ₹200-500 for guides, ₹100-200 for drivers (customary, not mandatory).

### Health & Safety
- **Water**: Tap water UNSAFE everywhere. Use bottled mineral water.
- **Food Safety**: Choose busy vendors for street food. Avoid raw salads from unknown stalls.
- **Vaccinations**: No mandatory vaccinations, but Hepatitis A and Typhoid recommended.
- **Medical**: Private hospitals available in all three cities. Carry basic first-aid kit.

### Weather by Month
| Month | Delhi (°C) | Agra (°C) | Jaipur (°C) | Notes |
|-------|-----------|----------|------------|-------|
| January | 7-20 | 7-22 | 8-22 | Peak season, fog possible |
| February | 10-24 | 10-26 | 10-25 | Ideal conditions |
| March | 15-30 | 15-32 | 15-31 | Warming, still comfortable |
| April | 22-38 | 22-40 | 22-37 | Getting hot |
| May | 27-42 | 27-44 | 27-41 | Extreme heat |
| June | 28-40 | 28-42 | 28-39 | Pre-monsoon heat |
| July | 27-35 | 27-36 | 26-34 | Monsoon begins |
| August | 26-34 | 26-34 | 25-33 | Heavy monsoon |
| September | 25-34 | 25-35 | 25-34 | Monsoon ending |
| October | 19-33 | 19-34 | 19-33 | Season begins |
| November | 12-28 | 12-29 | 13-29 | Excellent conditions |
| December | 8-22 | 8-23 | 9-23 | Peak season, fog possible |

### Transport
- **Delhi to Agra**: 230 km. Car: 3.5-4h (Yamuna Expressway). Gatimaan Express: 100 min.
- **Agra to Jaipur**: 240 km. Car: 4-5h (NH-21).
- **Jaipur to Delhi**: 280 km. Car: 4.5-5h (NH-48).
- **Local Transport**: Uber/Ola available in all three cities. Delhi Metro is extensive.

---
Verified by AsiaByLocals Licensed Historians. Updated February 2026.
